description: Repair repo-facing and planner-facing documentation on main so README navigation, review policy, taxonomy, CONTRIBUTING, and factory planner guidance agree on the ten curated Awesome List categories, live automation (make targets, Go checks, GitHub workflows), and manual review ownership—establishing one accurate source of truth before Phase 7 content seeding.

context.customerAsk: Finish the documentation reconciliation the prior phase-6-7-docs-convergence-cleanup batch was meant to land. Update README.md Contents/footer coherence (ten curated categories, Related Lists as curated category, Community not in Contents), docs/review-policy.md (automation exists via make targets, Go checks, and GitHub workflows; manual review owns scope, quality, and convergence), and factory/docs/overview.md (describe awesome-agent-factories, planner state files, actual customer ask; remove stale AI model reference website and documentation-site-pages-needed.md references). Keep README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/review-policy.md, docs/taxonomy.md, and factory/docs/overview.md consistent. Leave make check, go test ./..., and git diff --check clean. Do not start Phase 7 content seeding.
context.problem: Phase 6 templates and Phase 4–5 automation are on main, but a completed cleanup idea never landed its documentation changes: README Contents lists Community instead of reflecting curated categories, review-policy still claims automation is absent, and factory/docs/overview.md still describes the old AI model reference website and a nonexistent documentation-site roadmap file.
context.solution: Apply a narrow documentation-only repair: fix README Contents/footer semantics, rewrite review-policy to document live automation while preserving manual judgment ownership, repair factory planner overview for this Awesome List repository, align taxonomy and sibling docs on categories and workflow, and verify with make check, go test ./..., and git diff --check before the Phase 7 content gate.

acceptanceCriteria: README.md Contents lists Scope plus nine in-TOC curated sections (Theories through Examples and Templates), omits Related Lists, Contributing, and Community, and retains Related Lists as the tenth curated section heading
docs/review-policy.md documents live make targets, Go checks, and GitHub workflows while stating manual review owns scope fit, quality, convergence, and borderline judgments

factory/docs/overview.md describes Awesome AI Agent Factories, references docs/internal/customer-ask.md, checklist.md, and progress.txt, and removes stale AI model reference and documentation-site-pages-needed.md references
README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/review-policy.md, docs/taxonomy.md, and factory/docs/overview.md agree on ten curated categories and contributor/planner workflow
No Phase 7 README content seeding or unrelated governance rewrites are introduced
Quality gate: make check, go test ./..., and git diff --check all exit 0 from repository root

userStories.title: Converge README Contents with curated categories
userStories.description: As a contributor reading the list, I want the README table of contents to reflect the ten curated resource categories so I know where to place submissions without treating governance links as list content.
acceptanceCriteria: ## Contents includes anchor links for Scope and for Theories, Coordination Patterns, Frameworks, Protocols and Interfaces, Benchmarks, Research Papers, Blog Posts, Case Studies, and Examples and Templates in canonical order
## Contents does not include Related Lists, Contributing, or Community
## Related Lists remains present as the tenth curated category section
## Contributing and ## Community remain after curated sections; ## Community covers conduct and security only, not list entries
make check exits 0 after the README update
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update review policy for live automation with manual judgment ownership
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want docs/review-policy.md to describe what automation enforces today and what still requires human judgment so reviewers and contributors share accurate expectations.
acceptanceCriteria: Introduction no longer states automation does not run or that Phase 4 tooling is absent
Document live local automation: make check, make test/go test ./..., optional make links and make lint, with pointer to CONTRIBUTING.md GitHub Actions table
Document live GitHub workflow gates: ci.yml, link-check.yml, awesome-lint.yml, scheduled-maintenance.yml as read-only enforcement aligned with local commands
Checklist items claiming automated link checking is unavailable are updated to reflect make links, link-check.yml, and scheduled link checks
Document explicitly states manual review still owns scope fit, section-fit disputes, agent-factory relevance, quality bar, removal/relocation, and convergence judgments
Former Future automation (Phase 4) section reframed: Phase 4 checks and Phase 5 workflows are present; only not-yet-implemented enhancements remain future work
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Repair factory planner overview for this repository
userStories.description: As a meta-planner operating the agent factory, I want factory/docs/overview.md to describe this Awesome List repository and its planner state files so batch submission guidance matches reality.
acceptanceCriteria: Overview identifies the project as Awesome AI Agent Factories—a curated awesome list for agent-factory topics—not an AI model reference website
Read First list includes factory/factory.json, factory/workstations/ideafy/AGENTS.md, docs/internal/customer-ask.md, docs/internal/checklist.md, and docs/internal/progress.txt
Read First list does not reference docs/documentation-site-pages-needed.md or other nonexistent paths
Phase control text points to docs/internal/customer-ask.md for current phase authorization and the Awesome List build goal

Factory workflow sections (work types, workstation flow, batch submission, state inspection, repair) remain accurate with you docs agents / you docs batch-inputs and factory/docs/batch-input-example.json references
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Align taxonomy and cross-document category workflow language
userStories.description: As a maintainer preparing Phase 7 seeding, I want taxonomy and sibling policy docs to agree with README and CONTRIBUTING on categories and workflow so planners and contributors read the same rules.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/taxonomy.md lists the same ten curated README categories as CONTRIBUTING.md and does not describe Phase 4 automation as unimplemented when referring to current repository state
docs/taxonomy.md future-automation language distinguishes implemented Phase 4 checks and Phase 5 workflow gates from not-yet-started Phase 7 content seeding
CONTRIBUTING.md category list, README section headings, and docs/review-policy.md checklist name the same ten categories with matching headings
factory/docs/overview.md does not contradict contributor category or local-check guidance in CONTRIBUTING.md
No new README resource entries, issue-template edits, or unrelated governance rewrites are introduced
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Verify documentation convergence quality gate
userStories.description: As a planner running convergence review before Phase 7, I want end-to-end verification that the repaired documentation passes repository quality gates and contains no whitespace regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: From repository root, make check exits 0
From repository root, go test ./... exits 0
From repository root, git diff --check exits 0

README.md Contents, CONTRIBUTING.md categories, docs/taxonomy.md, docs/review-policy.md, and factory/docs/overview.md contain no contradictory claims about whether automation runs or what the ten curated categories are
No Phase 7 list entries are added
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
